subject: Fwd: meeting with Condren
body:

Darren K.  Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor

Fax: 212-750-0381

(http://food.aol.com/dinner-tonight?NCID=aolfod00030000000001)


From: "Steven Storch" <sstorch@samlegal.com>
Subject: meeting with Condren
Date: Fri, 02 May 2008 16:02:14 GMT
To: dkiesq@aol.com

We spent about an hour. It was quite cordial . Bottom line is they are
interested in talking about the fund and ABS losses but said that with
all the litigation and class actions swirling around about the common
stock he thought it unlikely they would discuss anything on that,
although he did say that any settlement on the other losses could
involve preservation of claims re the stock. He is going to get back to
me next week on their position on scope of further discussions. He said
that he expected they would want to sit down and see if we could get
something done. I did not put any numbers on the table. Nor did he. We
talked about the nature of the claims and the amounts that were invested
.

We have a draft complaint with which I am not yet happy. If it is not a
problem, I would like to have the weekend so that we can smooth it out
and provide a more finished product Monday . Please let me know if that
is going to be a problem.

 

David Anders and I are now trading messages. As soon as I hear from him
I will let you know.

 

Steven G. Storch

Storch Amini & Munves, P.C.
2 Grand Central Tower
140 E. 45th Street
New York, New York 10017
Tel: (212) 490-4100 ext 216
Fax: (212) 490-4208

Confidentiality Notice: The information contained in this e-mail and any
attachments may be legally privileged and confidential. If you have
received this e-mail in error, please notify the sender and permanently
delete the e-mail and any attachments immediately. You should not
retain, copy or use this e-mail or any attachment for any purpose, nor
disclose all or any part of the contents to any person. Thank you.

From:Steven Storch <sstorch@samlegal.com>
Subject:meeting with Condren
Date:Fri, 02 May 2008 16:02:14 GMT
To:dkiesq@aol.com

We spent about an hour. It was quite cordial . Bottom line is they are interested in talking about the fund and ABS losses but said that with all the litigation and class actions swirling around about the common stock he thought it unlikely they would discuss anything on that, although he did say that any settlement on the other losses could involve preservation of claims re the stock. He is going to get back to me next week on their position on scope of further discussions. He said that he expected they would want to sit down and see if we could get something done. I did not put any numbers on the table. Nor did he. We talked about the nature of the claims and the amounts that were invested .

We have a draft complaint with which I am not yet happy. If it is not a problem, I would like to have the weekend so that we can smooth it out and provide a more finished product Monday . Please let me know if that is going to be a problem.

 

David Anders and I are now trading messages. As soon as I hear from him I will let you know.
